title: Document always-on monitor UX
goal: Update Khazad-Doom docs and skill instructions around the simple always-on monitor path: users start monitor --latest wherever they want, while any harness starts daemon-owned runs normally.
acceptance: README explains TUI as terminal user interface and recommends khazad-doom monitor --repo . --latest as the harness-neutral live progress path.
README distinguishes watch as plain fallback, monitor as dashboard, and Pi extension as an optional adapter, not core workflow state.
The skill tells Pi to recommend or use monitor --latest for user-visible progress and not to rely on blocking --wait for real Pi runs.
khazad-doom run JSON includes enough information for any harness to present the monitor command without guessing environment-specific launch behavior.
Docs explicitly say Khazad-Doom does not auto-open external windows by default.
must_ask_if: The implementation would make Pi-specific UI required for normal monitoring.
